other_observers: None; I was not the original finder of this bird - it had been reported on cobirds.
description: The bird was flying around over the water, occasionally landing on the water.  It was noticeably larger than the numerous Bonaparte's Gulls, and comparable in size to Ring-billed Gulls.  When it was sitting on the water, I could see a narrow dark bill, black eye, small gray ear patch, and white crown, upper nape, and underparts.  It had a broad blackish collar at the nape/mantle transition, a somewhat scaly gray back, and a bold, black horizontal band (carpal bar) just above the water (as viewed when it was sitting on the water).  In flight, a bold black "M" pattern was obvious on the back and wings. The outer part of the M formed the leading edge of the wings. The inner part of the M had gray in front of it. Behind the M, the upper wing surface was white.  The under surface of the wings was light gray in front, white behind (low contrast between these two regions).  The white tail had a narrow black terminal band.Behaviors: The bird spent most of its time flying around over the water, and occasionally landed on the water. This behavior and its overall appearance supported its identification as a gull, but I did not use the behavior to help identify it to species.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenile
